rationale: Medicine L5 only where clinicians literally search for and cite by name: specific drugs, device platforms, named trial regimens, or biomarker-defined subgroups. Cardiology fellowships split by device generation + named drugs; oncology by molecular subgroup + named trial regimens; IBD/MS/derm by individual biologic; transplant by induction/maintenance regimen. Drugs that just landed (2024-2026 approvals) get their own leaves because they ARE the publication topic. Skip L5 where L4 is already the named entity (e.g. 'Lu-177 PSMA' L4 doesn't split — it IS the agent).

uncovered_concerns: Medicine L5 leans on the high-impact specialties — most surgical specialties stay at L4 because subspecialty procedures don't decompose into named entities that are themselves search-worthy.
Drug L5 leaves will drift as new agents approve. Recommend a quarterly review process.
